_meta.prototype: L2 per-term-per-verse finding store (formulate -> write -> correct)
_meta.grain: one finding per (term, verse); characteristic = typed term-in-verse
_meta.note: Read-only prototype, NOT the DB. tier values are the answer OR 'stated-silent' (verse does not resolve it) OR 'stated-unresolved' (escalated). Findings are drafts, corrected as evidence/clarifications accumulate.
_meta.tier_keys: T7.1=lexical, T1.2=kind(type), T1.4=mode(stem), T2=constitutional location, T3=faculty, T1.3=boundary

tiers.T2: HEART ("the dread your heart shall feel")

findings.provenance: mechanical (single-shade context) + T2 locus named in verse

tiers.T7.1: to fear -> dread (Qal; threat-object 'sword' -> dread shade)
to fear -> revere (Qal; covenant-obedience posture 'fear the Lord who brought you out')
Niphal -> to be feared / awesome (God as the feared one, in praise)

T7.1: to fear -> dread (Qal; fear of God-as-threat, dread/woe posture read in verse)

findings.provenance: clarification:fear-of-God-as-threat->dread (signal read in verse) — supersedes the induced god->reverence

corrections.reason: FORCED FINDING un-induced: 1Sa 4:7 'the Philistines were afraid ... Woe to us! ... who can deliver us from these mighty gods?' is dread of God-as-threat, not reverence. god-present alone does not resolve the shade; the dread posture, read in the verse, does.
corrections.clarification: REVOKE god-present->reverence (it INDUCES); apply fear-of-God-as-threat->dread where the verse shows a dread/woe posture

tiers.T7.1: to fear (Qal; shade fear/awe/reverence not settled by mechanical pass)

T7.1: to fear -> revere (Qal; God as 'you', covenant-obedience-life context)

corrections.reason: ESCALATED then RESOLVED by a new clarification: 1Ki 8:40 'that they may fear you all the days that they live' — God is the object (as 'you'), in a covenant-obedience-life frame -> reverence. The earlier escalate was a detection gap, not genuine silence.
corrections.clarification: ADD god-addressed-as-'you' counts as a god-object; covenant-obedience-life context ('fear you all the days they live') -> reverence
